title: pelazoneuron depilatum
intro_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Pelargonium sidoides (also known as Pelazoneuron depilatum) benefits athletes by modulating the immune response associated with the upper airway mucosa, increasing secretory immunoglobulin A levels and decreasing interleukin-15 and interleukin-6 levels.
side_effects: Pelazoneuron depilatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can also lead to gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In rare cases, improper use of the plant may cause cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations. It's essential to consult a medical professional before using Pelazoneuron depilatum for any health benefits to avoid these potential side effects.
